concept.title: Reuse, Keys, and Variant Management
concept.shortdesc: How to write content once and assemble it into multiple deliverables using conref, keyref, conkeyref, and DITAVAL filtering.

section.title: Why does content reuse matter?
section.p: Reuse eliminates duplication. Instead of copying content into multiple documents and maintaining every copy, you write content once and reference it wherever it is needed. When the source changes, every reference updates automatically at publish time.

ul.li: Consistency: Every document uses the exact same text for shared content
Reduced maintenance: Update once, publish everywhere
Translation savings: Reused content is translated once, not per-document
Quality assurance: Reviewed content stays reviewed across all uses

section.title: How does conref work?
section.p: The conref mechanism pulls content from one topic into another at publish time. It works like a symlink for content — the source topic contains the authoritative text, and referencing topics include it by ID.
This pulls the paragraph with id="safety-warning" from shared/warnings.dita into the current topic.

section.title: How do keys and keyref work?
section.p: Keys provide indirect references. You define a key in your DITA map, then reference that key in topics. This decouples content from specific file paths or values.

ul.li: Product names: <ph keyref="product-name"/> resolves to different names per deliverable
Cross-references: <xref keyref="install-guide"/> resolves to the correct target in each map
Variable values: Version numbers, URLs, and other changing values defined once in the map

section.title: What is conkeyref and when should I use it?
section.p: Conkeyref combines conref and keyref — it pulls reusable content through an indirect key reference rather than a direct file path. This is useful when the source file for reused content varies between deliverables.
section.codeblock:
<!-- Map defines the key: --> <keydef keys="shared-warnings" href="product-a/warnings.dita"/>
<!-- Topic references content through the key: --> <note conkeyref="shared-warnings/battery-warning"/>
section.p: By switching the key definition in a different map, the same topic can pull warnings from product-b/warnings.dita instead — without any change to the topic itself.

section.title: How does DITAVAL filtering work?
section.p: DITAVAL files include or exclude content based on attribute values. You mark content with attributes like audience, platform, or product, then use a DITAVAL file to control what appears in each output.
section.codeblock:
Log in as administrator to access this feature.

Contact your administrator for access.

section.title: What are the best practices for reuse?

ul.li: Always use keys for product names, version numbers, and URLs
Prefer keyref over conref when the referenced content may vary by deliverable
Store reusable content in a dedicated shared/ directory
Test all variants (DITAVAL combinations) before publishing
Document your key definitions in a warehouse topic or README
